{
  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
  "record_type": "coverage_assessment",
  "id": "senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-21",
  "name": "Senolytics coverage repair assessment",
  "track_id": "senolytics",
  "hallmark_id": "cellular_senescence",
  "assessment_type": "coverage_repair",
  "assessed_at": "2026-06-27T18:13:25Z",
  "coverage_verdict": "adequate",
  "coverage_confidence": "moderate",
  "summary": "The senolytics track has an auditable vertical slice across preclinical D+Q discovery/healthspan evidence, early human biomarker and pilot human studies, a phase 2 randomized bone trial, a 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, translation-review context, risk-of-bias records, and registry-only active trial-watch records. DKD and AD-risk human records now include PubMed abstract-located sample, dosing, timepoint, and effect details, and IPF has an abstract-located dosing-completion result plus corrected RCT sample size. These upgrades remain below source-snapshot-backed extraction grade, and the D+Q bone publication-table and supplement reconciliation remains incomplete.",
  "evidence_categories": [
    {
      "category": "human_interventional",
      "coverage_level": "adequate",
      "rationale": "Includes early D+Q human biomarker, randomized IPF pilot, phase 2 bone, and AD-risk cognition/mobility pilot records; DKD, IPF, and AD-risk now have at least abstract-located extraction for sample, dosing, timing, or effect details.",
      "source_ids": [
        "pmid-31542391",
        "pmid-36857968",
        "pmid-38956196",
        "pmid-40010154"
      ],
      "finding_ids": [
        "dq-dkd-human-senescent-cell-biomarker-signal",
        "dq-ipf-randomized-feasibility-tolerability-mixed",
        "dq-bone-rct-primary-null-subgroup-biomarker-signal",
        "dq-ad-risk-pilot-cognition-mobility-signal"
      ]
    },
    {
      "category": "animal_lifespan_or_healthspan",
      "coverage_level": "adequate",
      "rationale": "Includes foundational discovery work plus the old-mouse healthspan and post-treatment survival paper.",
      "source_ids": [
        "pmid-25754370",
        "pmid-29988130"
      ],
      "finding_ids": [
        "dq-senolytic-drug-discovery-preclinical",
        "dq-old-mice-healthspan-lifespan-positive"
      ]
    },
    {
      "category": "mechanistic",
      "coverage_level": "adequate",
      "rationale": "Cellular senescence targeting is anchored by the discovery source and translation review, but pathway-level extraction is not yet complete.",
      "source_ids": [
        "pmid-25754370",
        "pmid-32686219"
      ],
      "finding_ids": [
        "dq-senolytic-drug-discovery-preclinical",
        "senolytic-translation-review-boundary"
      ],
      "gap_note": "Future extraction should separate senescent-cell survival pathways, SASP markers, and tissue-specific clearance evidence."
    },
    {
      "category": "null_negative_or_limiting",
      "coverage_level": "adequate",
      "rationale": "The IPF, bone, and AD-risk records explicitly encode mixed, pilot, primary/subgroup, and feasibility limitations instead of flattening them into positive claims; the AD-risk record now includes the abstract-level MoCA and TNF-alpha estimates.",
      "source_ids": [
        "pmid-36857968",
        "pmid-38956196",
        "pmid-40010154"
      ],
      "finding_ids": [
        "dq-ipf-randomized-feasibility-tolerability-mixed",
        "dq-bone-rct-primary-null-subgroup-biomarker-signal",
        "dq-ad-risk-pilot-cognition-mobility-signal"
      ]
    },
    {
      "category": "safety_durability",
      "coverage_level": "thin",
      "rationale": "Safety-oriented records now include the D+Q bone registry aggregate counts, full-text adverse-event table extraction, an accepted structured headache preferred-term row, and retained locators for withdrawal, dosing cadence, and durability context. The remaining publication table rows, discontinuation details, and the DKD/IPF/AD-risk human safety slices still need row-level or source-specific extraction before safety synthesis.",
      "source_ids": [
        "pmid-38956196",
        "nct-04313634",
        "pmid-36857968",
        "pmid-40010154",
        "pmid-32686219"
      ],

        "senolytic-translation-review-boundary"
      ],
      "gap_note": "Run bounded extraction for the remaining D+Q bone adverse-event rows and discontinuation counts, then ingest or retain source snapshots for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety extraction before attempting cross-human safety synthesis."
    },
    {

{
  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
  "record_type": "result",
  "id": "dq-dkd-biomarker-positive",
  "name": "D+Q diabetic kidney disease senescence biomarker result",
  "source_id": "pmid-31542391",
  "study_id": "dq-diabetic-kidney-disease-pilot",
  "outcome_id": "dq-dkd-senescent-cell-biomarkers",

    "dq-dkd-human-senescent-cell-biomarker-signal"
  ],
  "intervention_ids": [
    "dasatinib-quercetin"
  ],
  "comparator": "Within-participant before-versus-after pilot design",
  "arm_details": "Oral dasatinib 100 mg plus quercetin 1000 mg for 3 days.",
  "sample_size": 9,
  "result_type": "descriptive",
  "effect": {
    "measure": "descriptive",
    "raw_text": "Abstract reports reduced adipose tissue senescent-cell burden within 11 days, including p16INK4A- and p21CIP1-expressing cells, senescence-associated beta-galactosidase activity, adipocyte progenitors with limited replicative potential, adipose macrophage/crown-like structure markers, skin p16INK4A and p21CIP1 cells, and circulating IL-1alpha, IL-6, MMP-9, and MMP-12; numeric effect sizes are not provided in the abstract."
  },
  "direction": "positive",
  "evidence_tier": "human_biomarker",
  "confidence": "low",
  "statement": "In an open-label N=9 diabetic kidney disease pilot, a 3-day D+Q course was followed by lower senescent-cell and SASP-related biomarker signals 11 days after treatment completion.",
  "time_horizon": "Before treatment to 11 days after completing a 3-day D+Q course",
  "caveats": [
    "Open-label N=9 biomarker study; do not interpret as clinical efficacy.",
    "Abstract-level extraction does not provide numeric effect sizes or p values for individual biomarkers."
  ],
  "tags": [
    "senolytics",
    "human",
    "biomarker"
  ],
  "maturity_status": "abstract_extracted",
  "provenance": [
    {
      "source_id": "pmid-31542391",
      "locator_type": "abstract",
      "locator": "PubMed PMID 31542391 abstract METHODS, FINDINGS, and INTERPRETATION",
      "status": "abstract_extracted",
      "note": "Abstract gives N=9, D+Q dose and timing, the 11-day post-treatment assessment, and directional biomarker decreases; it does not provide table-level numeric effects.",
      "source_snapshot_id": "snapshot-pmid-31542391-pubmed-efetch-2026-06-27"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
  "record_type": "result",
  "id": "dq-ad-risk-cognition-mobility-mixed",
  "name": "D+Q AD-risk cognition and mobility exploratory result",
  "source_id": "pmid-40010154",
  "study_id": "dq-ad-risk-cognition-mobility-pilot",
  "outcome_id": "dq-ad-risk-cognition-mobility",
  "finding_ids": [
    "dq-ad-risk-pilot-cognition-mobility-signal"
  ],
  "intervention_ids": [
    "dasatinib-quercetin"
  ],
  "comparator": "Single-arm before-versus-after pilot without a control group",
  "arm_details": "Dasatinib 100 mg plus quercetin 1250 mg for 2 days every 2 weeks over 12 weeks.",
  "sample_size": 12,
  "result_type": "quantitative_effect",
  "effect": {
    "measure": "mean_difference",
    "value": 1,
    "ci_lower": -0.7,
    "ci_upper": 2.7,
    "unit": "MoCA points",
    "raw_text": "Mean MoCA increased by 1.0 point (95% CI -0.7, 2.7) overall; among participants with the lowest baseline MoCA scores, MoCA increased by 2.0 points (95% CI 0.1, 4.0). Mean TNF-alpha changed by -3.0% (95% CI -13.0, 7.1), and TNF-alpha change correlated inversely with MoCA change (r=-0.65, p=0.02)."
  },
  "analysis": {
    "comparison": "Post-intervention versus baseline",
    "method": "Single-arm absolute and percent change estimates reported in PubMed abstract",
    "p_value": "correlation p=0.02",
    "effect_note": "Overall MoCA confidence interval crosses zero; subgroup and biomarker-correlation results are exploratory."
  },
  "direction": "mixed",
  "evidence_tier": "human_functional",
  "confidence": "low",
  "statement": "The AD-risk pilot reported an imprecise overall MoCA increase, a larger exploratory increase among participants with lower baseline MoCA scores, and an inverse TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation after 12 weeks of intermittent D+Q.",
  "caveats": [
    "Single-arm N=12 completion sample without a control group.",
    "Mobility endpoint values are not extracted from the abstract and need full article extraction.",
    "Subgroup and biomarker-correlation signals are exploratory."
  ],
  "tags": [
    "senolytics",
    "human",
    "pilot",
    "cognition",
    "mobility"
  ],
